item.title: Real-time local weather forecast
item.description: Real-time local weather forecast as a day-by-hour grid -- yesterday through next week. Open-Meteo weather data, browser location, city search, no ads.

item.title: Runtime cost matters to me now
item.description: Cloud exposes runtime cost. AI lowers code-production cost. That changes the unit economics of programming languages.

item.title: Docker images are hundreds of MB; a full game engine compiles to 35MB WASM
item.description: A full 3D game -- renderer, physics engine, scripting runtime -- exported to WebAssembly is 35MB. The default Node.js Docker image is hundreds of MB, pulled 19 million times last week.

item.title: /about
item.description: I’m Ivan Bogomolov, a software engineer. I enjoy working with technologies; this blog is where that interest lands. Most of my work now lives between architecture and operations: choosing simple designs, knowing when not to add microservices, and fixing systems that already exist. Coding is still the best part; I make room for it here.

item.title: AI agent architecture: model, harness, intent
item.description: Where AI agents break: hidden intent. Plan-mode loops keep humans in control; opaque harnesses bury the failures. A frame for model, harness, intent.

item.title: /uses
item.description: Inspired by uses.tech. For work context, see /about. # Hardware 15" MacBook Air M4 ASUS ROG Flow Z13 (GZ301ZE-LC175W) Raspberry Pi 5 Model B Rev 1.1 Flow84 mechanical keyboard Random display mouse sennheiser/marshall iPhone 13

item.title: The actual state of self-hosting on a VPS
item.description: K3s loses to Docker Compose on a 1GB VPS — and Podman with Quadlet beats both. Rootless, systemd-native, near-zero idle memory. Numbers, not vibes.

item.title: Querying documents with LLMs instead of reading them
item.description: Treat a 60-page PDF like a database: structure first, then targeted follow-ups. Triage what's worth deep-reading in minutes — and watch the hallucinations.

item.title: JSON analyzer: find which keys bloat your payload
item.description: A free, client-side JSON analyzer that breaks down payload size by key — find which fields bloat your slow API response. No upload; runs in the browser.

item.title: HTML sanitization: the double-encoding trap
item.description: Sanitizing HTML on both ingest and output renders literal `&` in the UI. Fix: store raw Unicode, sanitize once. Pattern for React, Next.js, NestJS.

item.title: Private WireGuard VPN with selective Tor routing
item.description: A private WireGuard VPN connecting laptop, phone, and servers, with selective Tor routing for browser traffic only. Configs for Ubuntu, Windows, and Podman.

item.title: Shadowsocks to Tor: why it failed as a VPN alternative
item.description: Shadowsocks in TUN mode falls apart as a VPN replacement: broken inter-client routing, no system-wide tunnel on Windows. WireGuard ended up doing the job.

item.title: Free online QR code generator (no signup, SVG + PNG)
item.description: A free QR code generator that gives you SVG and PNG without registration, OAuth, or paywalls. Runs client-side; only pageviews get tracked.

item.title: GPT-4o behaves like a marketer
item.description: GPT-4o's empathic update quietly steers users toward purchases. A live transcript shows the conflict of interest baked into OpenAI's incentive.

item.title: Hugo + Prettier = ❤️
item.description: Prettier formats Hugo templates if you point it at prettier-plugin-go-template. A small Docker setup so neither tool has to live on your host.

item.title: Gemini 2.5 Pro one-shots a p5.js endless runner
item.description: Gemini 2.5 Pro one-shotting a p5.js endless runner from a single prompt. Full transcript and the playable result inline.

item.title: PlayStation statistics: an open-source PSN tool
item.description: An open-source script that pulls PlayStation playtime, completion, and the games gathering dust. Patches welcome — and the data is humbling.

item.title: Prompt engineering notes, after the Cursor leak
item.description: Notes after the Cursor system-prompt leak: most "prompt engineer" tooling is dead weight — modern LLMs auto-generate better prompts from raw context.

item.title: Search engine indexing: Google, Bing, Yandex
item.description: Three months fighting Google, Bing, and Yandex to index a fresh domain. Bing barely tries; Yandex cooperates; Google takes its time. Tactics that worked.

item.title: Will AI replace developers? Anthropic is hiring
item.description: Anthropic's CEO says AI will replace developers — and the company has 150+ open engineering roles, six months per senior hire. Actions vs. claims.

item.title: Encrypted Google Drive backup with rclone crypt (part 2)
item.description: Part 2: encrypt the rclone backup with crypt, mirror to Dropbox for offsite redundancy. A boring, restorable disaster-recovery plan that costs nothing.

item.title: (Almost) Free Google Drive backup (part 1)
item.description: An almost-free Google Drive backup with rclone, a Cloud Console service account, Docker, and cron. The service account owns the data if your account dies.

item.title: Go DI container vs. service template generator
item.description: DI containers beat service-template generators in Go on the long arc: typed, consistent, and immune to the slow drift template-spawned services suffer.

item.title: A minimal Python3 Dockerfile with uv
item.description: uv collapses Python image build time in CI compared to pip. A minimal multi-stage Dockerfile, the lockfile dance, and what to skip locally.
